2605.18194.title: Beyond the Cartesian Illusion: Testing Two-Stage Multi-Modal Theory of Mind under Perceptual Bottlenecks
2605.18194.paper_summary: While Multi-Modal Large Language Models (MLLMs) demonstrate impressive capabilities in general reasoning, their embodied spatial intelligence remains hampered by a "Cartesian Illusion" - a reliance on text-based probability distributions that lack grounded, 3D topological understanding. This limitation is starkly exposed in multi-agent environments, which demand more than just scene perception; they require second-order Theory of Mind (ToM). Specifically, an Agent A must be able to infer Agent B's belief about the environment, governed strictly by Agent B's physical orientation and sensory limitations. In this paper, we probe the limits of two-stage spatial inference in MLLMs through a novel audio-visual task: requiring Agent A to predict Agent B's estimation of A's relative location. To solve this, we propose an Epistemic Sensory Bottleneck module that abandons rigid, rule-based coordinate transformations. Instead, we introduce an Anchor-Based Embodied Spatial Decomposition Chain-of-Thought (CoT). This guides the MLLM through a "geometric-to-semantic" projection, forcing it to first establish B's local coordinate system and then dynamically weight visual and auditory modalities based on whether A falls within B's visual frustum. Extensive evaluations reveal that while current MLLMs fundamentally struggle with spatial symmetry and out-of-view ambiguities (establishing a rigorous zero-shot baseline of 42% accuracy), our sensory-bounded reasoning chain robustly outperforms pure egocentric and allocentric baselines. By systematically benchmarking these perceptual bottlenecks, our work exposes the current limits of MLLM spatial reasoning and establishes a foundational paradigm for epistemic, modality-aware inference in Embodied AI.
2605.18194.paper_summary_zh: 雖然多模態大型語言模型（MLLMs）在一般推理方面展現了令人印象深刻的能力，但它們的具身空間智能仍受到「笛卡爾幻覺」的限制——依賴於缺乏具體、三維拓撲理解的基於文本的概率分佈。這一限制在多智能體環境中尤為明顯，這些環境要求的不僅僅是場景感知；它們需要第二階段的心智理論（ToM）。具體而言，代理 A 必須能夠推斷代理 B 對環境的信念，這完全受限於代理 B 的物理方向和感官限制。在本文中，我們通過一個新穎的音視覺任務探討 MLLMs 中兩階段空間推理的極限：要求代理 A 預測代理 B 對 A 相對位置的估計。為了解決這個問題，我們提出了一個認識性感官瓶頸模塊，放棄了僵化的基於規則的坐標變換。相反，我們引入了一個基於錨點的具身空間分解思維鏈（CoT）。這指導 MLLM 通過「幾何到語義」的投影，迫使其首先建立 B 的局部坐標系，然後根據 A 是否位於 B 的視覺錐內動態加權視覺和聽覺模態。廣泛的評估顯示，儘管當前的 MLLMs 在空間對稱性和視野外模糊性方面根本上存在困難（建立了一個嚴謹的零樣本基準，準確率為 42%），但我們的感官限制推理鏈在性能上穩健地超越了純自我中心和他中心基準。通過系統性地基準測試這些感知瓶頸，我們的工作揭示了當前 MLLM 空間推理的極限，並為具身人工智慧中的認識性、模態感知推理建立了一個基礎範式。
2605.18194.author: Yajing Zhou et.al.
